description: Affrication and palatalization of the alveolar nasal [n] to the voiced palato-alveolar affricate [dʒ]. This can occur through processes like yod-coalescence, where an [n] is followed by a palatal glide [j], or as a form of emphatic palatalization.

languageExamples.description: In the historical development of some Romance languages, the sequence /nj/ (or /ni/ followed by a vowel) often palatalized, sometimes passing through a [ndʒ] or [dʒ] stage before becoming a palatal nasal [ɲ]. For example, Latin 'vineam' -> *'vinja' -> Old Spanish 'vinna' /'viɲa/ may have had intermediate affricate stages in some dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
